steps.title: Identify the subject structure
steps.content: The sentence reads: “Hanseatic League member ______ was serendipitously located...” The blank must contain a noun phrase serving as the subject of the verb “was”. You need to pair “city” with “Rostock” to identify which city.

steps.title: Apply the rule for noun identification
steps.content:
When a common noun is directly followed by a proper noun for identification, no comma separates them: “city Rostock” is the correct form.

A, B, and C all introduce commas incorrectly—A between subject and verb, C unclosed, and B to mark “Rostock” as extra detail when it's essential identification.

That matches Choice D — city Rostock.
